2605.22455.title: Making the Discrete Continuous: Synthetic RAW Augmentations for Fine-Grained Evaluation of Person Detection Performance in Low Light
2605.22455.paper_summary: Real-world deployment of AI vision models is both fueled and limited by the data available for training and testing. Real datasets are sparse and uneven: long-tailed or unbalanced distributions hinder generalization, and the low number of samples in low density regions makes it hard to run evaluations. Synthetic data can fill these gaps, providing us with a way to sample the input space more continuously and improve data coverage for benchmarks. Focusing on the autonomous driving safety-critical case of pedestrian detection in the dark, we show how synthetic low-light samples can be used to better characterize the performance of a state-of-the-art object detection model as a function of the scene illumination. We use a synthetic RAW image augmentation technique to generate low-light samples that match the noise model of the camera sensor. Performance metrics on real and synthetic low-light data are similar, indicating that the AI model finds it hard to distinguish between them.
2605.22455.paper_summary_zh: 現實世界中 AI 視覺模型的部署既受到可用於訓練和測試的數據的推動，也受到其限制。<lb/>真實數據集稀疏且不均勻：長尾或不平衡的分佈妨礙了泛化，而低密度區域樣本數量少使得評估變得困難。<lb/>合成數據可以填補這些空白，為我們提供了一種更連續地抽樣輸入空間的方法，並改善基準測試的數據覆蓋率。<lb/>專注於自動駕駛安全關鍵的黑暗環境行人檢測案例，我們展示了如何使用合成低光樣本來更好地表徵最先進物體檢測模型的性能，這一性能取決於場景的照明。<lb/>我們使用合成 RAW 圖像增強技術生成與相機傳感器噪聲模型相匹配的低光樣本。<lb/>在真實和合成低光數據上的性能指標相似，這表明 AI 模型難以區分它們。
2605.22455.author: Valeria Pais et.al.
2605.22455.authors: Valeria Pais, Malena Mendilaharzu, Daniele Faccio, Luis Oala, Christoph Clausen, Bruno Sanguinetti
